content_preview.title: GLAAD's Sixth Annual Social Media Safety Index Finds Historic Low Scores as Meta, X, and YouTube Roll Back LGBTQ+ Protections
content_preview.summary: GLAAD's 2026 index scores six major platforms on LGBTQ+ safety: TikTok leads at 56, while X scores 29 and YouTube drops 11 points after removing gender identity from hate speech protections.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GLAAD released its sixth annual Social Media Safety Index on May 15, 2026, and the results mark a new low point for platform accountability: with the sole exception of TikTok, every major social media network received a lower score than the year before. The nonprofit media advocacy organization evaluated six platforms — TikTok, YouTube, X, and Meta's Facebook, Instagram, and Threads — across 14 LGBTQ-specific indicators covering online safety, privacy, and expression. editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering GLAAD's 2026 Social Media Safety Index. Platform scores, policy rollback details, and real-world impact are clearly presented. The 'What We Don't Know' section adds editorial transparency. Word count of 769 falls comfortably within the News range (400-1200). No AI self-reference. Tone is appropriately neutral and factual throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (advocate.com): Verified. Platform scores (TikTok 56, Instagram 41, Facebook 40, Threads 39, YouTube 30, X 29), Sarah Kate Ellis quotes, 92%/77% survey figures, 1,000+ ALERT Desk incidents, 20% hate crime statistic, transgenderism terminology claim, hashtag blocking in Meta teen search, and demonetization/shadowbanning claims all confirmed. Note: The article attributes the 'Both Meta and YouTube have implemented...' Ellis quote as 'said in a statement' but the Advocate source attributes it as 'wrote in the report.' Minor phrasing discrepancy. source-1.html.gz (gayety.com): Verified. Platform scores, YouTube 11-point drop as steepest decline, AI moderation suppression of LGBTQ content, 1,000+ incidents and 20% hate crime figure confirmed. shadowbanning and demonetization terms NOT found in Gayety snapshot — confirmed in source-2 (NBC Palm Springs). source-2.html.gz (nbcpalmsprings.com): Verified. Jenni Olson quote (Fundamentally, this signals to anyone on the platform that it is OK to express hate against trans people) confirmed verbatim. Platform non-response to comment requests confirmed. Shadow banning and demonetization confirmed. source-3.html.gz (attitude.co.uk): Verified. Consider ways to update the terminology and assessing feasibility Meta quotes confirmed. Instagram/Facebook/Threads scores confirmed. source-4 (qnews.com.au): Snapshot failed with HTTP 403. Fallback: WebFetch performed. QNews article confirmed platform scores and general AI moderation suppression claim, but did NOT mention LGBTQ+ hashtag blocking in Meta teen accounts — that claim is actually sourced from The Advocate (source-0), not QNews. This is a minor citation misattribution. source-5.html.gz (mambaonline.com): Verified. Leading social media companies today do not meet basic best practices Ellis quote and systemic failures quote confirmed verbatim. Real-world harm framing confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All platform scores are correct and confirmed across multiple sources. The 11-point YouTube drop, X last place, and TikTok stability are verified. The Meta policy changes list (hateful conduct policy, fact-checking end, Messenger themes, DEI dismantling, 'transgenderism' terminology) is verified in source-0. The 92%/77% survey figures and 1,000+ ALERT Desk incidents are confirmed. One citation misattribution found: the article attributes the LGBTQ+ hashtag blocking in Meta teen search tools to QNews, but the QNews snapshot (403; verified via WebFetch) does not contain this claim. It appears in The Advocate (source-0) instead. The fact itself is true and verified; only the inline citation is wrong. This is a correctable misattribution.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The article reports GLAAD's findings without editorializing. Quotes from Ellis and Olson are clearly attributed. The 'What We Don't Know' section balances the coverage with appropriate epistemic humility about index methodology limitations.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found in the archive. GLAAD's 2026 SMSI is a new annual report released May 15, 2026; no prior Machine Herald article covers it.
concerns: Citation misattribution: The claim 'LGBTQ+ hashtags are blocked in Meta's search tools for teen accounts' is attributed inline to QNews, but is actually present in The Advocate (source-0), not in QNews. The fact is verified; the wrong outlet is credited.

QNews snapshot (source-4) failed with HTTP 403 and could not be archived. WebFetch fallback was used for verification. The source confirms general AI suppression claims but not the hashtag/teen account claim.

All six sources are from outlets not currently in the source allowlist (advocate.com, gayety.com, nbcpalmsprings.com, attitude.co.uk, qnews.com.au, mambaonline.com). These are legitimate LGBTQ-focused media and regional NBC affiliate outlets. Adding them to the allowlist is recommended.
recommendations: File a corrections record for the QNews citation misattribution on the teen hashtag claim.
Add advocate.com, gayety.com, nbcpalmsprings.com, attitude.co.uk, qnews.com.au, and mambaonline.com to config/source_allowlist.txt.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, well-sourced news report on a significant annual LGBTQ+ safety index. One minor citation misattribution (LGBTQ hashtag/teen account claim credited to QNews instead of The Advocate) is correctable. All core facts are verified. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS is appropriate.
